## Global rules (apply everywhere)

> **For tenants and AI agents reading this**: these rules describe the
> Coding Capybaras boilerplate's architecture and tell AI assistants how
> to work in it safely. They apply equally whether you're a tenant
> customizing your downloaded copy or a contributor to the upstream
> boilerplate itself. The most important rule below is the region split
> (`/platform/` vs `/website/` vs `/product/`) — getting that right
> keeps upstream pulls clean and prevents you from accidentally
> rewriting something the boilerplate will overwrite on the next
> update. New here? Start with [the root README](./README.md) for setup,
> then read on for the conventions your AI tooling should follow.

This repo has **three regions**. Determine which region a change belongs in
before editing.

- **`/platform/`** — locked plumbing: auth, billing, email, payments, journey,
  config, admin. Maintained upstream by the Coding Capybaras boilerplate.
  Don't modify unless the user explicitly asks; local edits will conflict on
  the next upstream pull.
- **`/website/`** — the marketing surface (landing, pricing, legal, contact).
  Customize freely.
- **`/product/`** — the authenticated app (everything behind sign-in that
  isn't billing, account, or admin). Build freely.

`/app/` at the repo root is a **routing manifest** — thin shims that point
into `/platform/`, `/website/`, or `/product/`. Never edit `/app/` files
directly. Use `pnpm new:route <path>` to add a route (it creates the shim and
the page file together).

**Database tables follow strict prefixes**: tables defined in
`/platform/db/schema/` are prefixed `platform_`; tables in
`/product/db/schema/app.ts` are prefixed `app_`. Never reach into
`platform_*` tables from product code — use the public interfaces in
`/platform/lib/*` (documented in `/platform/INTERFACES.md`).

**Imports** go through path aliases: `@/platform/*`, `@/website/*`,
`@/product/*`. Don't use relative imports (`../../`) across regions.

**Dependency direction**: product → platform, never the reverse. The single
intentional exception is `/platform/db/client.ts`, which imports
`/product/db/schema/app` so there's a unified Drizzle client (one `db`
import that types both `platform_*` and `app_*` tables). Don't use that
path as license to violate the rule elsewhere — platform code still
shouldn't *query* `app_*` tables.

**Stack lock.** This boilerplate is Next.js 15 App Router, TypeScript,
Tailwind, shadcn/ui, Drizzle, Supabase (auth + Postgres), Stripe, Resend,
Sentry, and PostHog. Don't swap any of those out without an explicit
discussion — the platform plumbing assumes them.

**Naming.** Routes are kebab-case. React components are PascalCase. Database
tables use the `platform_` / `app_` prefixes above.

**Secrets live only in `.env.local`.** Never paste secrets into web forms.
`.env.local` is gitignored; if you ever need to confirm, run `git status`.

**See `README.md` at the repo root** for the human-facing overview (what
this is, how to run it, how to pull updates from upstream).

**If a request would require modifying files across regions, STOP and
confirm intent.** Cross-region edits are rare and should be explicit.

## Region: /platform/

**Read-only for normal product work.** Reference code here to understand
how things behave, but don't modify any file in this region unless asked
to "update the platform" or fix a platform bug. The platform ships from
upstream — local edits will conflict on the next pull.

When extending a platform feature (a new email template, feature flag,
payment provider), do the work here and update `/platform/INTERFACES.md`
to reflect any new exports.

The Stripe SDK is imported in exactly one file:
`/platform/lib/payments/stripe.ts`. Every other Stripe call goes through
the `PaymentProvider` abstraction. Same pattern for Resend:
`/platform/lib/email/index.ts` is the only file that imports `resend`;
every other sender uses `sendEmail()`.

Tests live next to the code they test (no separate `tests/` directory).

## Region: /website/

**Modify freely.** This is the marketing/landing layer.

Compose UI from shadcn primitives under `@/platform/components/ui/*`
(Button, Card, Input, Dialog, Badge, etc.). Don't reimplement those —
wrap them if you need variants.

Read configurable values (app name, primary color, logo, tagline) via
`@/platform/lib/config` — never hardcode them. Admin edits in
`/config/branding` flow through to the marketing site.

Marketing pages live in `/website/pages/` with thin routing shims under
`/app/(website)/`. The shared chrome lives at
`/website/pages/_website-layout.tsx`. The landing page, pricing, legal
pages, and contact page are already wired up — new marketing routes go
here too.

Don't import from `/platform/lib/*` internals — only through the public
interfaces in `/platform/INTERFACES.md`.

## Region: /product/

**Modify freely.** This is where the actual app is built.

**Database**: prefix new tables with `app_` and put them in
`/product/db/schema/app.ts`. Never touch `/platform/db/schema/`. Generate
migrations with `pnpm db:generate`.

**Routes**: use `pnpm new:route <path>` (e.g.
`pnpm new:route /app/(authed)/app/settings`). It creates the `/app/` shim
and the `/product/pages/` page file together. Never edit `/app/` files
directly.

**Platform features** (auth, billing, sending email, feature gating,
configuration, audit logging) are reached via the public interfaces in
`/platform/INTERFACES.md`. Do not import from `/platform/lib/*` internals
— those can change between boilerplate updates.

**Product-specific server libs** (helpers that aren't routes — a
third-party integration client, a product-only query helper) live in
`/product/lib/<feature>/`. Route handlers under `/app/api/*` can be thin
shims that call into these libs, keeping `/app/` a routing manifest.

Use shadcn primitives from `@/platform/components/ui/*`. Product-specific
components go in `/product/components/`.

## Forbidden patterns

- Modifying any file in `/platform/` without an explicit user request to
  update the platform itself. Local changes will conflict on the next
  upstream pull.
- Editing files in `/app/` directly. Always create routes via
  `pnpm new:route`.
- Committing secrets (`.env.local`, API keys, signing secrets) to git —
  `.gitignore` covers `.env*` files already.
- Querying `platform_*` tables directly from product code. Use
  `@/platform/lib/*` interfaces.
- Querying `app_*` tables from `/platform/` code. The dependency direction
  is product → platform, never the reverse.
- Importing the Stripe SDK anywhere except
  `/platform/lib/payments/stripe.ts`. Same for the Resend SDK and
  `/platform/lib/email/index.ts`.
- Sending email outside `sendEmail()`. All transactional sends go through
  the central helper so they land in `platform_email_log`.
- Using relative imports (`../../`) across regions. Always use
  `@/`-prefixed path aliases.
- Running `pnpm dev` from this agent — the user owns the dev server in
  their own terminal.
- Running `pnpm build` while the user's dev server is running. Both write
  to `.next/` and corrupt each other's compiled chunks. Check
  `pgrep -fl "next-server"` first; if anything's running, skip the build
  and rely on `pnpm typecheck` + `pnpm lint`.
- Setting `set -a && source .env.local && set +a` to load env vars. Empty
  placeholder values get exported into the shell and silently shadow
  `.env.local` for subsequent processes. Use `pnpm dev` (loads
  `.env.local` automatically) or
  `npx dotenv -e .env.local -- <command>` for one-offs.

## Required patterns

- App tables prefixed `app_`; platform tables prefixed `platform_`.
- Product schema in `/product/db/schema/app.ts`; platform schema in
  `/platform/db/schema/platform.ts`.
- Cross-region imports via `@/platform/*`, `@/website/*`, `@/product/*`
  path aliases.
- New routes via `pnpm new:route <path>` (never by editing `/app/`
  directly).
- Secrets only in `.env.local`. Never pasted into web forms; never
  committed.
- Configuration values (app name, branding, pricing copy, email-template
  subject/body, feature flag membership) come from
  `@/platform/lib/config`, not from constants in code.
- Server actions and route handlers always validate input with a Zod
  schema before doing anything else.
- Every webhook handler verifies the provider's signature before
  processing the payload — no exceptions, even in dev.
- All transactional email through `sendEmail()` from
  `@/platform/lib/email` (it logs every attempt to
  `platform_email_log`).
- Platform interfaces are documented in `/platform/INTERFACES.md` — if
  you add a public export, update the doc in the same commit.
